_doc: PSCraft per-run configuration. Everything org-specific lives here so the skill stays company/CRM/stack agnostic. Copy to <workdir>/config.json and fill. Leave stage_map/stage_order until you see the real stage labels in Phase 2, then rerun. PSCraft scores the TECHNICAL EXPLAINER on each call (see roles).
_org_domains_doc: Email domains of the SELLING org. Splits internal participants from external customers/partners and builds the roster keyed by email. Anyone on these domains is a candidate technical explainer; everyone else is external.

scope._doc: Optional narrowing. null = whole corpus. Stated plainly in the report caveats.
scope._sample_doc: For a scoped pilot: list of deal_ids (or account names) to run before the full corpus. Set null for full run.

terminology._stall_label_doc: The operator's word for the parking stage: 'Hold' / 'Stalled' / 'Nurture' / 'Parked'.
terminology._technical_win_doc: The operator's word for the craft-side milestone: 'Technical Win' / 'Tech Validation' / 'POC Passed'.

currency._doc: Discovered from the data, not assumed. style 'short' formats large numbers as K/M/B; style 'indian' uses lakh/crore.

ingest._doc: Override paths if the layout differs. *_columns map an export's real headers to canonical names (synonyms tried in order). POC/MAP/security/design inputs are DOCUMENTS (PDF/DOCX/MD/TXT) read in full by a model in Phase 3.5, never parsed by keyword. All optional; the run degrades cleanly when any are absent.

se_platform_columns._doc: Maps a Vivun / Prelay / Cuvama (or similar) export. All optional. A technical-win flag here is a CLAIM until a call corroborates it.

ingest._recording_doc: Where the transcript's recording URL is read from (transcript header field). 'no link' renders if absent.

stage_map._doc: Map each raw CRM stage label to a canonical PSCraft rung. Fill after Phase 2 prints the real labels. Canonical rungs: intro_qualify, tech_discovery, tailored_demo, poc_pov, tech_validation, proposal, negotiation, won, lost, stalled.

_stage_note: These canonical rungs drive the rubric's stage_expectations (the NA logic). A model infers each call's SE stage; if no CRM stage exists it infers from the call's own arc. proposal_negotiation in the rubric matrix covers both proposal and negotiation rungs.

stalled_definition._doc: Which deals count as stalled. derived_from='auto' lets link.py detect it; set 'config' to force.

technical_win._doc: How the craft-side outcome anchor is resolved. 'inferred' = a model reads the corpus (plus any POC criteria doc / SE-platform flag) and decides the state per rubric.technical_win.definition. If the SE platform or CRM carries a flag, name the column; a flag with no corroborating call moment is a CLAIM, never proof.

scoring_universe._doc: Which calls feed the technical-explainer SCORES (the rest are context only). PSCraft scores whoever carried the technical-explainer role on a call.

roles._doc: Phase 5 infers, per call, WHO carried the technical-explainer role (title-agnostic). overrides[] forces an archetype/level/exclusion for a person. archetype in {solution-engineer, seller-doing-technical, se-leader, ae-nontechnical, partner-external}. seniority (level) in {associate, mid, senior, principal, lead}; model-inferred if omitted. name_fixes consolidates display-name variants.

rubric._doc: Overrides on schema/rubric.template.json. Leave {} to use defaults. You can override dimension weights per archetype, role bars, or per-level seniority bars.

reporting._doc: Reporting levels. call, se (per technical explainer), and deal are ALWAYS produced. quarter/QoQ trend is produced ONLY if the corpus spans >= min_quarters distinct quarters (inferred from call dates); otherwise it is skipped with a stated caveat.

_min_calls_doc: Technical explainers with fewer scored calls than this are shown in a directional appendix, not the ranked leaderboard.
caveats: The corpus is calls/demos only. Async POC work, email, Slack, and in-person whiteboarding are not included; absence of a behavior in the corpus is flagged, not penalized.
Craft scores are kept separate from BOTH the Technical Win and the business (deal) outcome by design.
PSCraft scores the technical explainer on each call, whoever carried that role; a seller who did the technical explaining is recognized and scored for it.
